title: 8 Herbal Essential Oils For Gastritis
intro_desc: If you're dealing with gastritis, a painful and uncomfortable condition, herbal essential oils can provide relief. These oils are extracted from plants and have natural healing properties that can soothe and calm your stomach. One reason why herbal teas are effective in relieving gastritis is because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation and ease pain. For example, Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to alleviate nausea and digestive issues. Its active compound, gingerol, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and relax the stomach muscles. Another herb, Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, is also known for its anti-inflammatory properties and can help protect the stomach lining from acid damage. Meanwhile, Curcuma longa, or turmeric, contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. By incorporating these herbs into your diet, either through tea or essential oils, you can experience relief from gastritis symptoms and improve your overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains gingerol and shogaol, two active constituents that help reduce inflammation in the stomach. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which ease the symptoms of gastritis and promote healing in the digestive tract. Gingerol and shogaol also have a natural anti-spasmodic effect, which helps relax the stomach muscles and reduce nausea and vomiting. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the stomach, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with gastritis. Regular use of this essential oil may also help improve digestion and reduce the risk of future gastritis attacks.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Ginger roots (Zingiber officinale) and 2 cups of water.
Grate 1/4 cup of the ginger roots and put it in a saucepan.
Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring it to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 30 minutes. Strain the liquid and discard the ginger.
Let the liquid sit for 24 hours to allow the oil to separate. Collect the separated oil using a paper towel or a coffee filter.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. Glycyrrhizin helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often a contributing factor to gastritis. The essential oil also contains flavonoids, such as luteolin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids work together to soothe and calm the stomach, reducing symptoms of gastritis. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils can help alleviate gastritis symptoms and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil) in a clean glass container.
Add 10 tablespoons of dried roots to a cheesecloth or a coffee filter. Tie the cheesecloth or filter to create a small bag.
Place the bag in the carrier oil and store in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container every day.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container. Discard the solids.
Store the Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 2-3 drops under the tongue or mix with water to treat gastritis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains curcuminoids, such as curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach lining, a common cause of gastritis. Curcumin also has a protective effect on the gastric mucosa, preventing acid and enzyme damage, and promoting the healing of gastric ulcers. The anti-inflammatory properties of curcumin also help to reduce the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and LOX, which contribute to gastritis. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, curcuma longa essential oils can help to alleviate symptoms of gastritis and promote the healing of the stomach lining.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Turmeric roots (Curcuma longa).
Use a mortar and pestle to grind the roots into a fine powder.
Transfer 1/4 cup of the powder to a glass jar. Leave 3/4 cup for later use.
Add 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil) to the jar with 1/4 cup of powder.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. Strain the oil and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains compounds like anethole, limonene, and pinene, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which is common in gastritis. The essential oil also has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing irritation and discomfort. Anethole, in particular, has been shown to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, preventing damage from acid and enzymes. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of Foeniculum vulgare essential oils make it a potential natural remedy for gastritis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 1 cup of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil).
Combine the seeds and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Store it in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use within 6 months.
To use, mix 5-7 drops of the Foeniculum vulgare essential oil with water or a carrier oil before applying to the skin or using as a digestive aid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains camphor, borneol, and 1,8-cineole as its key bioactive constituents. These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and protect against oxidative stress. The camphor in Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles in the stomach, promoting better digestion and reducing spasms. The borneol and 1,8-cineole in this essential oil have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and reduce the risk of gastritis flare-ups. By addressing inflammation, oxidative stress, and infections, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils may be beneficial for individuals experiencing gastritis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers. Wash them with clean water.
Combine the leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar. Add 2 cups of water and let it sit for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container. Discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of glycerin and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the liquid mixture. Stir well.
Store the mixture in a dark glass bottle and let it sit for 7 days before using it. Shake the bottle daily.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains high amounts of menthol and menthone, two active constituents that have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis. The antioxidant properties of menthone also help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, menthol has a soothing effect on the digestive system, which can help calm stomach acid and reduce symptoms of gastritis. The combination of these properties makes Mentha x piperita essential oils a potential natural remedy for gastritis.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh peppermint leaves (Mentha x piperita), a clean glass jar, a dark glass bottle, and a carrier oil like coconut oil.
Combine 1 cup of fresh peppermint leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil in the glass jar. Stir well.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. This allows the peppermint oil to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle. Discard the solids.
Store the peppermint essential oil in a cool, dark place and use 5-7 drops in water or tea to help soothe gastritis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils contains cinnamaldehyde, cinnamyl acetate, and linalool as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation associated with gastritis. Cinnamaldehyde, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori, a bacteria that can cause gastritis. The antispasmodic properties of linalool and cinnamyl acetate can also help to relax the stomach muscles and reduce symptoms of gastritis, such as nausea and abdominal pain. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria, Cinnamomum verum essential oils can provide relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal essential oils for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 500g of Cinnamomum verum bark, 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil), and a glass jar for storage.
Dry the Cinnamomum verum bark in a low-temperature oven (50°C) for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind 100g of dried Cinnamomum verum bark into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground Cinnamomum verum powder (100g) with the carrier oil (1 cup) in a saucepan, and heat the mixture over low heat (50°C) for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the glass jar, discard the solids, and store the Cinnamomum verum essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains silymarin, a bioactive constituent that has anti-inflammatory properties. This property helps reduce inflammation in the stomach, a common symptom of gastritis. The flavonoids and polyphenols in silymarin also have antioxidant properties, which can protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. The essential oils' anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may also help soothe and calm the stomach, reducing symptoms of gastritis. Silymarin's ability to stimulate the production of stomach mucus can also help protect the stomach lining and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers and 2 cups of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil)
Combine the dried flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. This allows the oil to infuse with the plant's properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container. Discard the solids.
Bottled the resulting infused oil in dark glass bottles and store in a cool, dry place. Use 2-3 drops of the oil in a carrier oil for gastritis relief.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent gastritis from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. They can also help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, making it easier for food to pass through. This may help prevent irritation and inflammation in the stomach lining.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be soothing for gastritis, but drinking them daily may not be suitable for everyone. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, can help calm digestive issues, but others, like ginger and turmeric, can irritate the stomach in large quantities. Listen to your body and adjust your tea choices accordingly.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with gastritis symptoms, but it's hard to say exactly how long they'll work. Some people notice relief within a few days to a week. It really depends on the severity of the condition and the specific tea being used. It's best to drink them regularly to see any benefits.
faq_time: For gastritis, it's best to drink herbal tea when your stomach is calm. This is usually in the morning after breakfast or in the evening before bed. Drinking tea too close to meal times can irritate your stomach further. Choosing a quiet time helps your body digest the tea's benefits without discomfort.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for gastritis, especially if they have similar effects on the stomach. For example, if you're taking a medication to reduce stomach acid and you drink a tea that also has acid-reducing properties, it could increase the risk of side effects. This is because the combination of effects can be too strong.
faq_pregnant: When you have gastritis and are pregnant, it's a good idea to talk to your doctor about what teas are safe to drink. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, might help soothe your stomach. But it's also possible that certain herbs could cause more problems, like bleeding or contractions. It's best to be careful.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding with gastritis, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, can soothe stomach issues, but others, like pennyroyal and sage, might not be safe. Research the specific herb and its effects on breastfeeding before consuming it. It's always better to err on the side of caution.
title_2: Gastritis relief for competitive athletes: 8 essential oils to try
